subtitle: Generate strong, unique passwords with breach detection
clickToCheck: Click "Check Breach Database" to verify against known breaches
excludeAmbiguous: Exclude ambiguous characters (0, O, l, I, 1, |)
bestPractice1: Use at least 20 characters for administrative accounts (16 minimum for standard users)
bestPractice2: Include 3+ character categories (uppercase, lowercase, digits, special)
bestPractice3: Never reuse passwords across different accounts or services
bestPractice4: Use the breach check to verify the password has not appeared in known data breaches (HIBP k-anonymity - your password never leaves your machine)
bestPractice5: Enable "Must change at next logon" when resetting passwords for other users
bestPractice6: Avoid dictionary words, personal info, or common patterns (abc123, qwerty)
bestPractice7: Consider using a password manager for storing generated passwords
hibpDescription: Breach data is provided by haveibeenpwned.com, a free service created by Troy Hunt that aggregates data from publicly disclosed security breaches.
hibpDbSize: The database contains over 14 billion compromised credentials from thousands of data breaches worldwide.
hibpPrivacyDetail: Only the first 5 characters of the password's SHA-1 hash are sent to the API. Your full password never leaves your machine.
notFoundInBreaches: Not found in any known breach
